verses.text: During the siege Jeremiah is imprisoned for foretelling the city's fall and Zedekiah's capture. The truth cost him his freedom. Faithful preaching often brings the prophet into chains.
During the siege Jeremiah is imprisoned for foretelling the city's fall and Zedekiah's capture. The truth cost him his freedom. Faithful preaching often brings the prophet into chains.

During the siege Jeremiah is imprisoned for foretelling the city's fall and Zedekiah's capture. The truth cost him his freedom. Faithful preaching often brings the prophet into chains.
God tells him his cousin will come to sell him the field at Anathoth, and bids him buy it. The strange command comes. God's word directs even small affairs to a larger purpose.
God tells him his cousin will come to sell him the field at Anathoth, and bids him buy it. The strange command comes. God's word directs even small affairs to a larger purpose.
God tells him his cousin will come to sell him the field at Anathoth, and bids him buy it. The strange command comes. God's word directs even small affairs to a larger purpose.
Jeremiah buys the field, weighs the silver, and signs and seals the deed before witnesses. He acts in faith. To purchase land in a doomed city was a bold sign of hope.

He has Baruch store the deeds in a jar, for 'houses and fields and vineyards shall be possessed again in this land.' Hope is sealed up. The buried deed declared that God would yet restore His people.
He has Baruch store the deeds in a jar, for 'houses and fields and vineyards shall be possessed again in this land.' Hope is sealed up. The buried deed declared that God would yet restore His people.
He has Baruch store the deeds in a jar, for 'houses and fields and vineyards shall be possessed again in this land.' Hope is sealed up. The buried deed declared that God would yet restore His people.
Jeremiah prays, owning God as Maker of heaven and earth: 'there is nothing too hard for thee.' He confesses God's power. Faith rests on the God for whom nothing is impossible.

He recalls the signs in Egypt and the gift of the land, which they entered but did not obey. History is rehearsed. God's past mercies expose the people's long ingratitude.
He recalls the signs in Egypt and the gift of the land, which they entered but did not obey. History is rehearsed. God's past mercies expose the people's long ingratitude.
He recalls the signs in Egypt and the gift of the land, which they entered but did not obey. History is rehearsed. God's past mercies expose the people's long ingratitude.
He recalls the signs in Egypt and the gift of the land, which they entered but did not obey. History is rehearsed. God's past mercies expose the people's long ingratitude.
He marvels: the siege-mounts are raised, the city is as good as taken, yet God said, 'Buy thee the field.' Faith and sight seem at odds. He brings his perplexity honestly to God.
He marvels: the siege-mounts are raised, the city is as good as taken, yet God said, 'Buy thee the field.' Faith and sight seem at odds. He brings his perplexity honestly to God.
God answers, 'Behold, I am the LORD, the God of all flesh: is there any thing too hard for me?' His power settles all. The God of all flesh can restore what seems hopelessly lost.
God answers, 'Behold, I am the LORD, the God of all flesh: is there any thing too hard for me?' His power settles all. The God of all flesh can restore what seems hopelessly lost.
God affirms the city will fall for its provocations, idolatry and sin from its founding. Judgment is just. The long record of rebellion has earned the coming ruin.
God affirms the city will fall for its provocations, idolatry and sin from its founding. Judgment is just. The long record of rebellion has earned the coming ruin.

God affirms the city will fall for its provocations, idolatry and sin from its founding. Judgment is just. The long record of rebellion has earned the coming ruin.
They built high places to Baal and even passed their children through the fire, which God 'commanded them not, neither came it into my mind.' Their sin was monstrous. Such horrors were never of God.

Yet God will gather them from every land, and 'they shall be my people, and I will be their God.' Restoration is sure. Beyond judgment lies the renewed covenant bond.
Yet God will gather them from every land, and 'they shall be my people, and I will be their God.' Restoration is sure. Beyond judgment lies the renewed covenant bond.
Yet God will gather them from every land, and 'they shall be my people, and I will be their God.' Restoration is sure. Beyond judgment lies the renewed covenant bond.

He will give them 'one heart, and one way' to fear Him, make 'an everlasting covenant,' and 'rejoice over them to do them good.' His joy is in blessing. God delights to plant His people in lasting good.

He will give them 'one heart, and one way' to fear Him, make 'an everlasting covenant,' and 'rejoice over them to do them good.' His joy is in blessing. God delights to plant His people in lasting good.
As surely as He brought the evil, He will bring the promised good, and fields shall be bought again. Restoration is certain. The God who fulfils His threats will surely keep His promises.
